title: Harden merge route test assertions: detail message + positive batch spy
analysis: Two test gaps in test_cluster_review.py need hardening, both in the merge route test classes. (1) test_returns_404_for_missing_cluster (line 543) asserts status_code==404 but never checks resp.json()['detail']. The route at review.py:408-412 already returns `f"Cluster {first_missing} not found"` so the string 'nonexistent' will appear in the detail when cluster_ids=['c-1','nonexistent']. Adding the assertion is a one-liner. (2) test_merge_uses_batch_fetch (line 943) monkeypatches get_activity_cluster to raise (proving the old N+1 path is NOT taken) but never positively asserts get_activity_clusters_by_ids IS called. The fix is to wrap the real method with a call-counting spy via monkeypatch and assert call_count >= 1 after the request. Both are test-only changes to a single file; no implementation code needs modification.

steps.description: Add detail message assertion to test_returns_404_for_missing_cluster: after `assert resp.status_code == 404`, add `assert 'nonexistent' in resp.json()['detail']` to verify the 404 error identifies the specific missing cluster ID.
Run tests to confirm step-1 assertion passes (implementation already returns 'Cluster nonexistent not found' at review.py:409). No code change needed — this step verifies green.

Add positive batch spy to test_merge_uses_batch_fetch: use monkeypatch to wrap CatalogDB.get_activity_clusters_by_ids with a call-counting spy (save original method, replace with wrapper that increments a counter list then delegates to original). After the POST request, assert the spy's call count >= 1.
Run tests to confirm step-3 spy assertion passes (merge route already calls db.get_activity_clusters_by_ids at review.py:405). No code change needed — this step verifies green.

design_decisions.decision: Use substring check `'nonexistent' in resp.json()['detail']` rather than exact string match
design_decisions.rationale: Task description specifies this form. It's resilient to minor wording changes in the error message while still verifying the missing ID is surfaced.

design_decisions.decision: Implement batch spy via a closure counter + monkeypatch wrapping the real method, not unittest.mock
design_decisions.rationale: The existing test already uses pytest monkeypatch (line 957). A closure-based counter (`calls = [0]`, wrapper increments then delegates) keeps the pattern consistent with the file's style and avoids adding unittest.mock as a new import.

design_decisions.decision: Spy wraps the real method (delegates to original) rather than replacing it
design_decisions.rationale: The test already asserts on the merge result (status 200, correct cluster_id, correct clip_ids). The spy must delegate so those downstream assertions continue to pass.

reuse.how: Same pattern used for the N+1 trap; reuse for the batch spy wrapper on get_activity_clusters_by_ids
Already used by both tests for seeding clusters; no new helper needed

reuse.where: Common FastAPI test pattern, not yet used in this file for 404 detail checks
reuse.how: Simple dict access on the JSON response body
